responseMessageContents.text:
The user is saying "DO 1" which refers to the option I proposed in my previous message:

> 1. **Push to a personal remote** — I can add a second remote (e.g. a GitHub repo you own) and push to it. That way you can clone from GitHub externally. Just share the repo URL.

So the user wants me to push the code to a GitHub repository. But they haven't provided a GitHub repo URL yet. I need to ask them for the GitHub repository URL.

Wait, let me re-read the conversation more carefully. The user said "DO 1" — meaning they want option 1: push to a personal remote. But I need them to provide the GitHub repo URL.

Let me ask for the URL.
To set that up, I need the GitHub repo URL you want to push to. Do you have one ready, or should I help you create one? Just share the URL (e.g. `https://github.com/your-username/cownit.git`).
